continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends New Jedi Order era, from Vector Prime and the first Yuuzhan Vong invasion through The Unifying Force and the war settlement. Excludes Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Disney canon outcomes except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and later post-NJO Legends unless a later deck is active.

content.fact: Yuuzhan Vong weapons can surprise Jedi because they do not behave like conventional tools in the Force or combat.
content.injection: In first encounters, let living weapons and Force-perception gaps create real danger. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Early Yuuzhan Vong invasion.
Respect story-position bounds from Vector Prime / Invasion Begins to Ithor Devastated.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
content.notes: Generated development Lorecard pending human review.

entries.title: Dovin Basals Affect Missiles and Shields

context.label: New Jedi Order / Yuuzhan Vong War

content.fact: Dovin basal gravity manipulation changes how missiles, shields, and starfighter tactics work.
content.injection: Do not apply standard shield/laser dogfight assumptions unchanged to Vong ships. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

content.fact: Targeting yammosks can disrupt Yuuzhan Vong coordination and change battle momentum.
content.injection: During the Yuuzhan Vong War, if a yammosk is identified, treat it as a high-value command-and-control target; damaging or killing it can plausibly disrupt Vong coordination and shift battle momentum.
